reasoning: The involvement of HHS Secretary RFK Jr., who specifically directed the CDC in April 2025 to review and halt its fluoride recommendation, strongly suggests a change was imminent. While typical government reviews take years, the new administration demonstrated rapid action when the FDA moved to remove prescription fluoride supplements from the market by May 2025. This shows a high willingness to execute anti-fluoride policies quickly. However, a KFF article in August 2025 still referred to the "current HHS/USPHS guidelines" recommending 0.7 mg/L, suggesting the formal recommendation may still technically be on the books or the website may not have been fully purged of the recommendation by July 1, 2025. Given the explicit pressure from HHS, it is more likely than not that the CDC's official recommendation page was pulled, suspended, or modified by the July 1 deadline, but bureaucratic inertia keeps the YES probability non-trivial.
